summary: Matched Icicle Mountain stage function grIceMt_801F686C in src/melee/gr/gricemt.c, with associated symbol/split metadata and data cleanup. Review feedback focused on preserving HSD_ASSERT/HSD_JObj helper forms and keeping ordinary strings inline rather than routing through extern char symbols.

key_files.role: Main source change: matched grIceMt_801F686C, converted local data definitions, adjusted StageData/StageCallbacks, used HSD_ASSERT and HSD_JObjSetTranslateY in final excerpt, and retained a PAD_STACK tactic.
Header surface changed; metadata reports two deleted lines, but loaded diff excerpt does not show the exact header hunk.

Updated gricemt-related symbol sizes, scopes, and data classifications, including grIm_803B825C, grIm_803E4068, grIm_803E40B0, grIm_803E4544, grIm_803E46F8, grIm_803E4800, and several local lbl_ string symbols.
Adjusted gricemt rodata/data/sdata split end addresses.

what_changed: PR body states one matched function: grIceMt_801F686C in gricemt.c for Icicle Mountain.

grIceMt_801F686C was implemented as background-platform setup: captures ground state, initializes stage flags, scans terrain-row table grIm_803E4068 three times for distinct row ids, accumulates Y offsets through grIceMt_801F993C, positions three background GObjs, and clears GObj slots on the fallback path.
The diff excerpt shows grIceMt_801F686C using three natural for loops over ICEMT_FIELD_MAX and HSD_ASSERT checks at lines 600, 609, 619, 636, 637, 641, 642, 646, and 647.
The diff excerpt shows use of HSD_JObjSetTranslateY for the three platform JObjs rather than the reviewed Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateY form.

The source diff expands and reclassifies gricemt data arrays and structs, including GrIm825C, IceMtRowData, S16Vec3 table data, grIm_803E4544, StageCallbacks, StageData grIm_803E4800, and several small GrIm588 constants.
The diff excerpt adds order_sdata2 and order_data0 helper functions that reference float literals, S32_TO_F32, __FILE__, and "i<ICEMT_FIELD_MAX" for ordering pressure.
symbols.txt was updated to reflect smaller split ranges, changed object sizes, local/global scope changes, and string/object classifications for gricemt data.
splits.txt shortened gricemt .rodata, .data, and .sdata end addresses.

The PR body reports build and objdiff verification: main.dol SHA1 OK, strict build with --require-protos and --map links cleanly, grIceMt_801F686C at 100.0%, and no fuzzy regressions in adjacent functions/TUs.
A bot report recorded seven new matches for main/melee/gr/gricemt, including .data, .sdata2, .rodata, .sdata, grIceMt_801F686C, grIceMt_801F8CDC, and grIceMt_801F9ACC.

smart_moves.text: Kept the recovered repeated row-selection behavior as natural for loops over ICEMT_FIELD_MAX rather than unrolled repeated blocks.
Restored HSD_ASSERT/HSD_JObjSetTranslateY style in the final diff excerpt after review objected to raw __assert expansions and fake helper use.
Moved ordinary asset path usage into StageData as inline string literal "/GrIm.dat" instead of maintaining a separate char array in the shown final diff.
Updated config symbol and split metadata alongside source changes, keeping data ownership and section ranges aligned with the matched unit.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching stage code with repeated table scans, first try natural loops with the exact counter and bounds before preserving generated-looking repetition.
Reviewer feedback in this PR supports keeping HSD_ASSERT rather than expanding to ternary __assert forms when the macro can be used.
Reviewer feedback in this PR supports keeping ordinary strings inline rather than introducing extern char declarations solely for matching, even if the inline literal gives a slightly lower match.
Header/helper inline evidence matters for jobj operations: prefer HSD_JObjSetTranslateY when that is the identifiable source operation rather than a fake helper used to steer codegen.

naming_conventions.convention: Use short role names such as gobj and jobj for clear local object pointers; final diff changes bg_gobj to gobj in grIceMt_801F686C.
Keep address-style grIm_* data names where semantic meaning is not proven; the PR defines and reclassifies many grIm address-style symbols rather than inventing broad semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK(0x10) appears in grIceMt_801F686C as a retained stack-shape tactic.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Loaded PR evidence reports successful build/objdiff verification, but does not explain why PAD_STACK was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: order_sdata2 and order_data0 dummy helper functions were added to reference literals and ordering-sensitive values.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This looks like data-order pressure; PR body reports full-unit verification, but the loaded evidence does not include reviewer discussion accepting this as general style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Source and config changes were paired with symbol/split updates to make gricemt .data, .rodata, .sdata, and .sdata2 match.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Useful for this PR, but not enough evidence to generalize specific symbol sizes or ownership beyond this file.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.comment: Don't replace HSD_ASSERT wiith __asserts if it can be avoided

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.comment: dont use extern chars, just unline the actual strings (even if that means a slightly lower match)

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: The data in this file is a total mess. I'm going to clean it up a little at least.

follow_up_queries: Find other review comments saying not to replace HSD_ASSERT with __assert.
Find PRs that introduced extern char declarations for strings and were asked to inline literals.
Search gricemt.c for order_sdata2/order_data0-style data ordering helpers.
Search stage TUs for PAD_STACK retained with objdiff evidence.
Search Icicle Mountain grIm_* data definitions for address-style symbols that might need stronger ownership evidence.

notes: PR metadata, PR body, review comments, changed-file counts, and a substantial diff excerpt are loaded.
raw_diff.diff is truncated, so the exact gricemt.h deletion and later gricemt.c hunks are not visible in the provided slice.
Verification claims come from the PR body and bot comment; this intake did not run builds or objdiff.

Review comments show intermediate code with raw __assert, Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateY, and extern char declarations; the final diff excerpt shows HSD_ASSERT and HSD_JObjSetTranslateY in grIceMt_801F686C, but the full final state is not fully visible because the diff is truncated.
Data-order helper functions are visible in the diff excerpt, but loaded evidence does not include reviewer rationale or acceptance as a reusable tactic.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer HSD_ASSERT over raw __assert expansion when possible
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2707, review explicitly objected to replacing HSD_ASSERT with ternary __assert expansions in gricemt.c. The later diff excerpt shows HSD_ASSERT forms in grIceMt_801F686C.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not introduce extern char symbols for ordinary strings just to improve match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2707, review explicitly said to inline actual strings instead of using extern chars, even if this gives a slightly lower match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural loops can express repeated stage table scans
accepted_candidate_records.text: grIceMt_801F686C uses three for loops over ICEMT_FIELD_MAX to select distinct row ids from grIm_803E4068, matching the PR body's description of successive scans.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2707 as an example for assert macro preservation
source_update_candidates.text: Use PR #2707 as an example under assert-report-macros/header-inlines: reviewer rejected replacing HSD_ASSERT with raw __assert expansions in gricemt.c when HSD_ASSERT could be retained.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards already contain this rule; this PR provides a concrete review-backed example.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2707 as an example for inline string literals over extern char symbols
source_update_candidates.text: Use PR #2707 as an example under no-string-literal-symbol-regression/literals-and-data-ownership: reviewer said to inline actual strings instead of using extern chars, even if the match is slightly lower.
source_update_candidates.reason: The current standards already cover string literal regressions; this PR adds explicit reviewer wording about accepting a lower match to keep literals inline.

source_update_candidates.title: gricemt.c data layout is sensitive and was reviewed as messy in PR #2707
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2707 changed many gricemt data definitions and config symbol/split records. A reviewer noted, 'The data in this file is a total mess. I'm going to clean it up a little at least.' Curator should verify whether a path fact is useful for future gricemt.c data work.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-scoped warning; should not be promoted without curator checking current source history and final cleanup state.

rejection_notes: Do not promote order_sdata2/order_data0 as accepted general style; the diff shows them, but loaded evidence does not include reviewer approval or rationale beyond match claims.
Do not infer that all grIm_* address-style symbols have final semantic ownership from this PR; the slice only shows this PR's symbol/split adjustments.
Do not treat the PR body's verification as independently reproduced by this intake.
